mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
This step installs the Google Cloud SDK, which includes the gcloud command-line tool necessary for managing GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to access gcloud commands.
> Initialize the gcloud environment and authenticate.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-cloud-logging --enable-cloud-monitoring --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the min/max nodes as per your requirements.
> This enables logging and monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent disks.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This step upgrades the GKE cluster to the latest version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that kubectl cannot reach the GKE API server, possibly due to network issues or incorrect kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this operation'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user account does not have the necessary IAM permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient resources or issues with the node's configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs using 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if there are quota limits exceeded in your GCP project.

**Fix:**
Check your GCP quotas and adjust as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE features such as Autopilot mode and enhanced security policies.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new security policies and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, confirming that your GKE cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and authenticated.
